REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS KELLY HENSLEY, LEFTOVERS, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KELLY HENSLEY, LEFTOVERS, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NICOLET E. CORLISS OF VLA&A LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-934-6102 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. EDWARD J. RODZEWICH OF THE STRATMAN PATTERSON & HUNTER, TEL. NO. 1-510-457-3460 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HENLEY;
ATTY. LORI B. FELDMAN, TEL. NO. 1-415-492-2120 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HENSLEY AND LEFTOVER, LLC APPEARING BY TELEPHONE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF TONY BOWLES' motion for leave to amend his complaint is granted in part. Plaintiff is granted leave to file his proposed first amended complaint but must delete all allegations therein pertaining to punitive damages. The addition of the three new claims is strongly supported by the policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. However, the addition of punitive damages allegations would prejudice defendants since the trial is less than two months away. Moreover, the punitive damages allegations do not appear to be well pled and plaintiff has not adequately explained why he didn't seek to allege punitive damages earlier. Request for admissions is deemed served today and with 10 days leave to respond. Defendant to prepare form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
